data.content:
Here's my analysis of the build infrastructure:

## Issues Found

### 1. No `Directory.Build.props` — Heavy Duplication
All 5 projects repeat these properties identically:
- `TargetFramework`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors`

### 2. No Central Package Management (`Directory.Packages.props`)
Package versions are scattered across projects with **version drift**:
| Package | Core/Api | Services | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---------|----------|----------|------------|-----------|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | 8.0.0 | **8.0.1** | — | — |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | — | — | 17.10.0 | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | — | — | 2.8.1 | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | — | — | 2.8.1 | **2.5.6** |

### 3. Duplicated Custom Target
The `PrintBuildInfo` target is copy-pasted into all 3 src projects.

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` Property
All 3 src projects set `<DocumentationFile>` identically.

### 5. Test projects repeat boilerplate
Both test projects independently list the same 4 test-infrastructure packages.

---

## Recommended Structure

```
repo-root/
├── Directory.Build.props          ← shared properties for ALL projects
├── Directory.Build.targets        ← shared targets (PrintBuildInfo)
├── Directory.Packages.props       ← central package versioning
├── src/
│   └── Directory.Build.props      ← src-specific (DocumentationFile)
├── test/
│   └── Directory.Build.props      ← test-specific (IsPackable, test pkgs)
```

**Root `Directory.Build.props`** — eliminates duplication from every `.csproj`:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Root `Directory.Build.targets`** — single copy of custom target:
```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

**Root `Directory.Packages.props`** — enables Central Package Management:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`src/Directory.Build.props`**:
```xml
<Project>
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**`test/Directory.Build.props`**:
```xml
<Project>
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Result: Slimmed-Down Project Files

After this refactoring, each `.csproj` contains **only what's unique**:

```xml
<!-- src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

```xml
<!-- test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests/ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Summary of Benefits

| Concern | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Change TFW | Edit 5 files | Edit 1 file |
| Package version drift | Possible (and present!) | Impossible |
| Add new test project | Copy 11 boilerplate lines | Just add `ProjectReference` |
| Custom build targets | 3 copies | 1 copy |

Would you like me to implement these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture of the build infrastructure. Let me analyze the issues:

1. **No `Directory.Build.props` or `Directory.Build.targets`** - Every project duplicates common properties
2. **Duplicated properties across all projects**: `TargetFramework`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors`
3. **Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property** in all 3 src projects
4. **Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` target** in all 3 src projects


5. **No `Directory.Packages.props` for centralized package management** - Package versions are hardcoded in each project with inconsistencies like `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` varying between `8.0.0` and `8.0.1`, and test SDK versions differing across projects.

6. **Test projects are duplicating common test infrastructure** - The same test packages appear repeatedly across different test projects, and solution folders aren't being used to organize source versus test code.

I'm thinking through what recommendations would help address these structural issues, focusing on consolidating package management and improving project organization.
